methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Claros, a Seed-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Daniel Kultran and Grant Verstandig, has raised $30M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers (including Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) and 1 public comparable (LNZA), all operating in advanced energy and climate technology.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $30M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding $75M. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables valued between $110M and $200M, with a median of $150M. This reflects recent Series A valuations for Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

The two methods produce a triangulated range with a low of $64M, median of $112M, and high of $173M. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively at the low end, while the peer-median method pulls the range substantially higher, reflecting the valuations commanded by later-stage climate-tech peers. Key uncertainties include the timing and structure of Claros's next financing round, capital intensity relative to peer cohorts, and whether the current peer comp set adequately reflects the company's specific technology and market position within the broader frontier-tech landscape.
